scope: R4 sequence world model (realenv/seq_worldmodel.py) + first held-out-image result (runs/dockerfs/seq-worldmodel.json). Adversarial pass BEFORE write-up, per standing constraint.
method: One independent adversarial reviewer. It re-encoded nothing new but reproduced the headline exactly from the cached frozen embeddings (wm=0.560, wm_no_history=0.418, retrieve_by_cmd=0.395, copy=0.097 on the 1500-step smoke) and generated the per-verb breakdown the shipped smoke JSON lacked. Threat classes probed: latent leakage, trivial-echo (cd), metric shortcut/degeneracy, capacity confound on 'history helps', standardization/foil fairness, gen-twin geometry, plain bugs.
headline_verdict: The WM-beats-lexical-memory claim on unseen systems is REAL and survives the echo confound. Content verbs (ls+cat, ~65% of steps): WM 0.484 vs retrieve_by_cmd 0.254 vs no_history 0.215 (+0.23 over lexical memory) on the smoke; the full 3-seed run confirms ls 0.544 / cat 0.579 for the WM vs 0.156 / 0.360 lexical. Two required fixes + one unearned claim, all addressed below.

findings.title: No-leakage claim referenced a unit test that did not exist
findings.detail: The causal mask (encode) and even/odd interleaving (collate; pred[:,0::2]) are provably correct — reviewer perturbed obs_0 and measured 0.000000 change in the t=0 cmd prediction, nonzero at t>=1. But the claimed committed leakage test did not exist; only cache-order asserts were present. No regression guard.
findings.resolution: FIXED. Added tests/test_seq_worldmodel.py with a perturbation-based no-leakage regression test (past cmd predictions invariant to future obs corruption), a self-only/no-history invariance test, and retrieval-metric calibration tests. All 4 pass.

findings.title: cd is a trivially-echoable verb and inflates the single headline number
findings.severity: HIGH for the headline scalar, LOW for the scientific claim
findings.detail: 100% of cd steps have empty output + exit 0, so render_obs = 'cwd=<target> exit=0' — the target is a substring of the 'cd <path>' command. cd is 28% of steps and carries ~0.196 of the 0.560. The WM does NOT exploit it (wm 0.792 < no_history 0.878 on cd), but the aggregate banks it. per_verb_breakdown/VERBSET is correctly designed to expose this, but the shipped smoke JSON omitted the by_verb block.
findings.resolution: FIXED. Full run persists by_verb in every result JSON; added content_retrieval (ls+cat only) as the honest headline. Reporting content-verb accuracy, never the echo-inflated scalar alone.

findings.title: 'History helps' rests on a transformer-vs-MLP capacity confound
findings.detail: wm_no_history is a 3-layer MLP while the WM is a 4-layer transformer; the +0.14 aggregate (and large ls/cat per-verb gaps) conflate genuine history use with better function approximation. Mixed depth-vs-position evidence (ls accuracy rises with sequence position; cat flat-to-declining). The MLP is not a clean history ablation.
findings.resolution: RESOLVED — history earned. Matched-capacity control (SAME transformer, self-only attention): content verbs (ls+cat) FULL 0.558 vs MASKED 0.296 => history gain +0.262±0.005 over 3 seeds; ls +0.32, cat +0.19; cd +0.00 (echoable, no history needed). The gain is concentrated exactly where a real world model predicts (content verbs) and absent where predicted (echoable cd), so the advantage is genuine history use, not function-approximation capacity. runs/dockerfs/seq-history-ablation.json.

findings.title: Metric is sound (attempted-break results)
findings.detail: Verb-conditional-mean predictor scores 0.027 ≈ chance on same-verb foils (cannot win by matching verb/length/dominant-token). Shrinkage toward mean is PUNISHED (0.2*true -> 0.221, 0.1*true -> 0.082), so the WM is not gaming MSE-retrieval with low-variance outputs. Standardized true-obs norms ~26.4 ≈ sqrt(768); predict_mean 0.020 ≈ 1/64 is real calibration. Ties handled by strict inequality.
findings.resolution: No action — evidence the retrieval metric is trustworthy.

findings.title: Gen-twin (JEPA vs recon) is a cross-geometry comparison
findings.detail: jepa ranks by MSE in a 768-d continuous space; recon ranks by BCE over a 4000-d multi-hot bag. Different geometries with different intrinsic retrievability, so 'JEPA beats recon by X' is not a clean apples-to-apples objective comparison. Full run: jepa 0.639 vs recon 0.526 (+0.113).
findings.resolution: REPORTED WITH CAVEAT. The generative twin is a legitimate, non-strawman baseline (0.526, well above chance); JEPA's +0.11 is suggestive but not leaned on as a headline given the geometry asymmetry.

findings.title: Minor: standardization stats include the dev split; retrieve_by_cmd cosine-select then MSE-score
findings.detail: Standardization uses full-train per-dim mean/std (incl. dev) — a tiny train->dev optimism leak that does NOT touch the held-out-image milestone (val standardized with train stats, applied identically to all predictors). retrieve_by_cmd selects nearest by command cosine but is scored by latent MSE — mild mismatch but it returns a full-variance real sample and still beats the WM on cd, so not unfairly handicapped.
findings.resolution: Acknowledged; no material effect on the held-out claim.

believability_checklist.history_helps_needs_same_capacity_masked: PASS (content ls+cat: full 0.558 vs matched-capacity masked 0.296, +0.262; cd gain +0.00 as predicted)
